source.title: Rethinking preventing class-collapsing in metric learning with margin-based losses

confidence_rationale: ENGINEERING COUNSEL ONLY -- not architectural authority. The paper proves theoretically that under reasonable noise assumptions, margin-based metric-learning losses tend to project all samples of a class onto a single point, regardless of intra-class structure ('class collapse'). This is a documented failure mode of learned-embedding training in multi-class settings, directly applicable to the option-B / option-C training risk for SD-049 Phase 2. Confidence at 0.74 because the paper is ML/DL not biological -- it speaks to engineering hazards under option B, not to whether option B is biologically correct. ICCV 2021 acceptance gives it peer-review weight as engineering counsel.
failure_signatures: Class collapse: under triplet-style or contrastive losses with reasonable noise assumptions, all samples of one class project to a single point in embedding space, regardless of within-class diversity. For SD-049 Phase 2 under option B, this would manifest as: novelty cells with high familiarity and low familiarity collapsing to the same z_resource, even though the agent's behavioural response should differ.

Mitigation: each sample selects its nearest same-class counterpart in batch as positive element rather than averaging across positives. This is a non-trivial training-loop modification; if Phase 2 picks option B without implementing it, class collapse is the likely failure mode at V3-EXQ-514.

Architectural counter-evidence to option B / option C: if the engineering hazard of class-collapse cannot be mitigated within the Phase 2 timeline, option A (one-hot, deterministic, no training loop) becomes the engineering-conservative default.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Under reasonable noise assumptions, prevalent embedding losses in metric learning -- such as triplet loss and contrastive loss with margin -- tend to project all samples of a class with various modes onto a single point in the embedding space, resulting in a class collapse that renders the embedding ill-sorted for fine-grained classification or retrieval. The authors propose an adaptive sampling modification where each sample selects its nearest same-class counterpart in a batch as the positive element, allowing multiple sub-clusters per class.
mapping.ree_translation: If SD-049 Phase 2 chooses option B (learned embedding) or option C (hybrid with embedding component), the canonical training-loop hazard is class collapse: the encoder collapses all instances of e.g. 'novelty' cells to the same point in embedding space, losing the within-type structure (familiar novelty vs unfamiliar novelty). This is the engineering counterpart of the EXQ-166b/c/d historical pattern Phase 2's phased-training protocol must defend against. Under option A (one-hot identity slot + magnitude), there is no embedding to collapse -- the architecture is collapse-immune by construction. Under option B/C, the Phase 2 training loop must include explicit anti-collapse mechanisms (adaptive sampling, regularisation, or supervised auxiliary losses).
mapping.mapping_caveat: ENGINEERING COUNSEL, NOT ARCHITECTURAL AUTHORITY (per the implement-substrate skill rule on Layer 7). This paper does not say anything about whether biological identity coding is sparse or distributed -- it only says that learned-embedding training is fragile in specific known ways. The verdict cannot use this paper to argue against option B as a biological architecture; it can only use it to argue that option B's Phase 2 implementation cost is higher than option A's because of training-loop hazards.
mapping.source_context: ML/DL paper; ICCV 2021 (peer-reviewed) plus arXiv preprint 2006.05162. Fine-grained image retrieval benchmarks (e.g. CUB, Cars-196). Metric learning context: train an embedding to make same-class items close and different-class items far apart. Failure mode demonstrated theoretically and empirically with intra-class diversity.

confidence_components.notes: ICCV 2021 is a top-tier peer-reviewed CV venue. Mapping fidelity moderate because the failure mode (class collapse in metric learning) is one of several training hazards REE Phase 2 may face -- it is not the only one, and not all of them apply to all option-B implementations. Transfer risk substantial: this is engineering counsel, not biological evidence, and it should never override a biologically-grounded architectural choice. It is included as a Phase 2 implementation hazard to defend against, not as an argument against any particular architecture.
